home.faq1Q: What online divination tools does ososn offer, and which should I use?
home.faq1A: We offer Four Pillars (Bazi), Plum Blossom, Liu Yao divination, Qimen Dunjia and more. Bazi reveals your life pattern and decade-long fortune cycles. Plum Blossom answers one specific question at a time. Liu Yao excels at judging how events unfold and their final outcomes. Qimen Dunjia reads timing, direction, and strategic choices. Each system draws from classical texts and can be used complementarily.
home.faq2Q: What information do I need for online charting?
home.faq2A: Four Pillars and Qimen Dunjia require an accurate birth date, time, and location for precise charting. Plum Blossom and Liu Yao do not require birth data — just focus on your question. We recommend saving your birth profile first so it auto-fills during readings, eliminating repetitive entry and reducing input errors.
home.faq3Q: How accurate is the chart data?
home.faq3A: All charts are computed locally using deterministic algorithms: Bazi follows standard stem-branch and solar term rules; Plum Blossom adheres to the original classical text; Liu Yao uses the three-coin casting method from traditional texts; Qimen Dunjia follows classical palace arrangement methods. Interpretations are based on chart structure from multiple angles. Core chart data (stems, branches, stars, hexagrams, palaces) is fixed and unchanging.
home.faq4Q: What if I don't know my birth hour?
home.faq4A: Four Pillars requires an accurate birth hour to determine the Hour Pillar. If unknown, you can still view partial information using year, month, and day only, or try Plum Blossom or Liu Yao — these tools don't depend on birth data. For Qimen Dunjia asking about current matters, you can also use the present time to cast a chart.
home.faq5Q: What's the difference between Bazi and Liu Yao?
home.faq5A: Bazi is destiny analysis — it reveals your innate pattern and fortune cycles from birth time, answering 'What kind of person am I and how is my fortune?' Liu Yao is divination — it answers specific questions through coin casting, such as 'Will this succeed and what is the outcome?' They complement each other: Bazi sees long-term trends and personality; Liu Yao judges specific matters and timing.
home.faq6A: All profile data is stored only in your browser's localStorage and never uploaded to any server. Chart calculations happen entirely on your device. During interpretation, only structured chart data (e.g., stems, branches, hexagram patterns, palace information) is transmitted — no personally identifiable information like your name. You can delete all data anytime in Settings.
home.faq7Q: Are these online divination tools free?
home.faq7A: Yes, all online charting and interpretation tools on ososn are completely free to use. There are no paywalls and no usage limits. You can perform unlimited readings for yourself, family, and friends anytime, with all features equally available.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: Privacy
privacy.section1Content: ososn uses a pure client-side architecture. All your personal data (including birth information and profile names) is stored only in your browser's localStorage. We do not upload this data to any server and cannot access it on your device.
privacy.section2Content: When you use the interpretation feature, your chart information (stems, branches, ten gods, fortune cycles, etc.) is forwarded to the interpretation service provider through our server proxy. We do not persistently store this information on our servers; we only act as a real-time request relay.
privacy.section3Content: We do not use any third-party tracking cookies. If you have configured analytics tools such as Baidu Analytics, data collection follows the privacy policy of the corresponding service provider.
privacy.section4Content: You can delete individual profiles at any time on the 'My Profiles' page, or clear your browser's localStorage to delete all data. We cannot delete data stored on your local device on your behalf.
privacy.section5Content: If you have any questions about this privacy policy, please contact us through the channels provided on the website.
privacy.seoDesc: ososn Privacy Policy. User data is stored only in the local browser. Interpretation requests only transmit chart data without personally identifiable information.

fuckClaude.title: Are you a Claude "China user"?
fuckClaude.subtitle: One click scans your browser environment locally and scores how strongly it resembles a China-user profile.
fuckClaude.toolDesc: Scan your browser timezone, language, Chinese fonts, and locale to see if Claude Code might flag you as a China user.
fuckClaude.ready: Hit start scan to wake the mascot.
fuckClaude.noHits: No strong China signals matched. Low risk.
fuckClaude.signalsSubtitle: Six locale fingerprints, weighted to a 0–100 risk score.
fuckClaude.howP1: When Claude Code is pointed at a proxy endpoint via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, public reverse-engineering reports found it reads your operating-system timezone and the proxy hostname, then hides the verdict inside the system prompt with Unicode steganography — the date separator and four look-alike apostrophes in the "Today's date" line encode whether you look like a China user.
fuckClaude.howP2: A web page cannot read everything Claude Code can, but the key signal is identical: this tool reads the same OS timezone, then adds five more browser-visible locale fingerprints — UI language, installed Chinese fonts, Intl locale, UTC+8 offset and emoji style — into a weighted score. Signals scoring ≥0.25 count as hits; bands are Low 0–30, Medium 31–60, High 61–100.
fuckClaude.privacyBody: Every check runs locally in your browser — your scan results never leave your device. None of the detected signals are ever sent.
fuckClaude.footerDisclaimer: For reference only, based on public reverse-engineering reports. Not an official statement or advice. Original project on

faq.q1: Does Claude really check my timezone?
faq.a1: According to public reverse-engineering reports, when Claude Code talks to a non-official endpoint it reads the OS timezone and proxy hostname, and steganographically encodes the result into its system prompt. The timezone this page reads via Intl.DateTimeFormat is the very same OS timezone.
faq.q2: Is this score the exact check Claude runs?
faq.a2: No. Only the system timezone maps one-to-one onto Claude's reported mechanism. The other five signals are common Chinese-environment fingerprints that correlate with it, so treat the score as an estimate, not a verdict.
faq.q3: How do I lower my score?
faq.a3: Switch your OS timezone away from China zones such as Asia/Shanghai, move zh-CN off the top of your browser language list, and avoid routing Claude Code through proxies whose hostnames contain flagged domains or AI-lab keywords.
faq.a4: No. Every check runs locally in your browser and none of the detected signals are ever sent anywhere.

zipingBazi.aiEmpty: Submit the form to receive an AI interpretation

zipingBazi.methodologyIntro: Zi Ping Bazi, also known as Four Pillars destiny analysis, uses the heavenly stems and earthly branches of the birth year, month, day and hour as four pillars, taking the day stem as the native and inferring life patterns through monthly command, five-element generation and restraint, and ten-god relationships. The method is attributed to Xu Ziping of the Song dynasty and is regarded as the orthodox system of Bazi divination.
zipingBazi.methodologyCard1Desc: The day stem is taken as the Day Master, representing the native self. The other three pillars unfold ten-god relationships around it, forming symbolic systems of family, career and wealth.
zipingBazi.methodologyCard2Desc: The monthly branch is the outline that governs the strength of the five elements. The pattern is determined by the main qi penetrating to the stems, setting the level and favorable direction of the chart.
zipingBazi.methodologyCard3Desc: Major luck cycles start from the month pillar: forward for yang males and yin females, backward for yin males and yang females, with each cycle spanning ten years.
zipingBazi.methodologyCard4Desc: After analyzing the strength of the Day Master, the elements that support or restrain it to achieve balance are favorable gods, while excessive draining or overwhelming elements are harmful gods.
zipingBazi.methodologySource: The above is summarized from classic Zi Ping texts such as Yuan Hai Zi Ping, San Ming Tong Hui and Zi Ping Zhen Quan, for cultural study and metaphysics enthusiasts only.

remedy.liusha: Liu Sha in the {direction} direction indicates emotional disturbance and restlessness. Avoid using it as bedroom or romance corner; use metal decorations, cool style and keep it ventilated and dry.
